subject: resource-closure
claim: The frozen asset ledger declares 452 bounded source asset records plus two separately evidenced runtime aliases, yielding 454 required source-to-runtime mappings.
limitations: This source-stage claim freezes mappings and provenance only. Physical runtime presence, byte equality, and reverse reachability are implementation numerators proved by later typed evidence.

subject: home-density
claim: The selected anonymous Singapore/USD source context records 27 homepage modules, seven product rails, and 157 unique product identities.
limitations: This is one dated anonymous context and does not represent every personalized Amazon homepage.

subject: catalog-identity
claim: The frozen implementation denominator enumerates 191 product identities that a candidate must know and route locally.
limitations: The source stage does not claim the candidate already implements them; known and reachable remain separate from rich, purchasable, review-backed, and comparable coverage.

subject: pdp-evidence
claim: The frozen rich-PDP denominator contains 14 product identities, including 11 source-direct PDP identities tracked separately.
limitations: A later full-suite artifact must prove these identities render rich PDPs; all other reachable products must disclose a sparse evidence boundary.

subject: transaction-quotes
claim: The frozen purchasability denominator contains 49 product identities expected to expose at least one complete server-owned quote.
limitations: The source stage freezes identity and quote boundaries only; no unobserved variant cross-product, seller, inventory depth, delivery promise, or price is inferred.

subject: cross-category-browse
claim: The retained source evidence spans multiple non-storage departments and preserves a 133-card placement target for bounded cross-category browsing.
limitations: Card placement may repeat identities and is not a product, PDP, or purchase numerator; candidate implementation is verified later.

subject: deals
claim: The dated Deals capture directly records ten current deal cards and their source assets.
limitations: Any additional local Deals placements must reuse separately supported product facts and may not be described as observations from the dated Deals page.

subject: reviews
claim: The frozen review-backed denominator contains 13 product identities; source aggregates and local authored rows must remain visibly separate.
limitations: A route for another product is not source review coverage, and local rows never become source facts.

subject: comparison
claim: The frozen comparable-product denominator contains 39 product identities and requires complete sibling-variant selection identity.
limitations: Eligibility and same-family limits are candidate semantics to be proved by the full suite, not a direct source observation.

subject: cart-semantics
claim: The P0 invariant requires same-product sibling variants to remain distinct while exact repeated selections may merge.
limitations: The source stage freezes the rule and its positive/negative test references; it does not provide an implementation numerator.

subject: variant-semantics
claim: The P0 variant invariant permits only complete supported quotes and requires incompatible values to be disabled before purchase.
limitations: The candidate must not invent unavailable labels or a Cartesian option product; later browser and full-suite evidence prove behavior.

subject: identity
claim: The frozen identity journey requires unknown-identifier registration continuation, local verification, and non-enumerating recovery with explicit local or SMTP-adapter outcomes.
limitations: Real delivery is never inferred from local simulation; browser and full-suite evidence provide the implementation numerator.

subject: payment
claim: The frozen failure and recovery journeys require deterministic decline, a safe approved-method retry, and no duplicate order on repeated placement.
limitations: No real card data, authorization, capture, or funds movement belongs to this offline scope.

subject: aftercare
claim: The frozen order invariant requires monotone cancellation, shipment, delivery, return, and refund transitions with immutable placement facts.
limitations: Carrier, delivery, receipt, and refund remain local deterministic simulations and require later typed browser/full-suite evidence.

subject: offline-runtime
claim: The frozen default local-only release profile forbids remote browser/resource requests and requires all 454 runtime mappings to resolve locally.
limitations: This is a source-stage acceptance requirement, not a claim that a candidate census already passed. Network evidence provides the browser/resource numerator; opt-in external SMTP delivery remains an uncertified external-effect adapter outside this claim.

subject: scope-boundary
claim: The purpose freeze assigns bounded local semantics to shopping-adjacent entry points while Prime Video remains an explicit placeholder.
limitations: The shopping purpose is prioritized, and no deep Prime Video ecosystem fidelity is claimed.
